go.

And, um, and

know righteousness that.

right now, I just don't think it's realistic that we're going to publish anything this week.

Okay, yeah, that's totally fine.

I mean, for on the accuracy standpoint, considering the new bill, we can rework the outlines, like just in Atlas, the platform that we use to generate content, we can, like, use the bill or any other resources that you have, like around how it's going to change tags.

And then use that and basically have the workflow kind of address those changes or at least use language that is like, okay.
This is how it's been done so far, but, you know, things are unpredictable right now, or something, or, you know, add something to that effect.

And so, I don't think we can, I don't think it's a good idea to publish posts that are like, oh, things may change.

So it's better not to publish than to publish something that isn't fully accurate.
Yeah.
And in general, I don't, yeah, I don't think couching the blog post in, like, hand-wavy language is, I don't think that speaks to our voice.
And so, that won't align, I think, with our voice principles.
So I think we need to be, like, on point, you know, clear, being spoken, and direct.
I think if we're going to write a blog post about uncertainty, shouldn't be a blog post about...

And that's a different kind of blog post, not doing here.
So just stepping back, it's not a you problem.
It's a like the law changed.
And it's a we, because it's changed, we, we shouldn't publish anything right now.
So I am 100% sure from the team that our first couple pieces of content are accurate.
So let me work on that.
That will take my few days.
I will work on that.
So that's like one bucket.
I don't think we should change the kind of blog post that we write or how we, I don't think we should be more hand-waving.
Okay.
All right.

Yeah.
So that's what I said.

Right?
Okay, that's fine.
This is not your fault.
This is our fault.

it's like still happening.

So as you can see like this, so it does have a reviewing step.
yearszy to Thank you.

then what we'll do is I'll add...

So we can set that up.

Uh-huh.
And then whenever something, and then the tax person on my team will go in, make the changes for tax accuracy.
When they're done, they'll, they'll move it to final review.
Then it should do a Slack to me, like in that channel, but it can add mention me.
Yeah.
I don't
And then when I move it to ready to publish, maybe then you, yeah, you should feel free to just put it in the CMS and publish it in whatever timeline you think makes sense.
Okay.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Okay.
All right.
Great.
So that's that out of the way.
I should have, like, the two new blogs ready by end of week for your review.
So that should be good to go.
Okay.
Okay.
That sounds good.
Yeah.

So the one thing I wanted to kind of bring up around some additional content ideas that I had that we don't have to execute right now, but I wanted to bring it up as, like, just a suggestion to have on your site, which is, like, there's two, which are playbooks for different industries.
Um, um, um, um,
And the SMB cohort and then doing like a tax risk assessment that could also be put up on your site.
And this is something I could work with the dev team to build in your new CMS once you do like migrate to it.

Okay.
What's the task group?
What do you mean by test risk assessment, Chris?
So let's see, like.
So there are these, these are more like consumer related.
So just want to look crap.
Okay.

Yeah.
Yeah.
So like gives you like an indication of whether you need like an expert.

Yeah.
I think.

I mean, they wouldn't have to be like super long.
was thinking like, you know, maybe just do like, make it more like straightforward, use like checklists and like incorporate, like maybe some.

Okay.

found a couple, um, I just need to dig in my history and send them over.

Um, so that resonates.
Um, Um, but let me spend some time thinking about it.
Yeah.
Yeah, okay, sure.

I was just talking to, like, for example, even with, like, when I was talking to Anjum about startups, you know, he mentioned how a lot of, like, startups forget about the Foreign Disclosures Act or they don't file their Section 83B election in time.

Great.

They're called WebStacks, and they're not, you know, in a similar.
There were there nature lot you.

Industry to you, they're a web development agency, but they use Sanity, and one thing about Sanity is that it allows you to create these components of, like, call to actions, like lead magnets that you can very easily add to a blog post or any other part of your site.

So I wanted to show you, like, this is like a blog that we have published on their site, and they have, like, these CTAs, for example, like, it's a blog about developer involvement in the involvement in the design process, and they have these CTAs that are, lead them to their design capabilities page, and this is something that, like, I has, we got really good results.

We were tracking, like, click-through rates to these capabilities pages from blogs, and the more customized they were, the more personalized they were to a specific topic, the better results we got, obviously, so that's just...

I wanted to surface that with you as you're thinking about the CMS and web development, because I think it's very helpful, and it's also helpful on our end when we're publishing, we can very easily plug them in without having to flag someone on your team and be like, hey, can you please add a CTA or a lead magnet or whatever?
I see, and so it basically adds to your own, it adds to landing pages about your specific product that are like, related enough to the blog post.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
Got it, okay.
Yeah, yeah.
So it's like, and then they have like a book and intro call at the end of the blog.

So you can really customize it.

Okay, that's cool.

mean, I think right now, you know, the first two blog posts, the only CTA is at the bottom in the last paragraph.

Also, how do you feel?

So, yeah.

I'm not a developer.
Okay.

And yeah, I think that's it.

Okay.

I'm, uh,

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy, review progress, and discuss next steps for Town/GrowthX collaboration.](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=96.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Recent tax bill changes have impacted content accuracy; publishing delayed until review](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)
  - [New review process established: tax review → final review → ready to publish](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)
  - [Continue generating blog posts (2 this week, 4 next) to build a content backlog](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
  - [Exploring new content ideas: industry playbooks, SMB cohort, tax risk assessment tool](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)

## Topics

### Content Progress and Challenges

  - [Calibration blog feedback incorporated; tone updated to be more plain-spoken](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=108.0)
  - [CMS updated, startup tax deduction blog staged but on hold due to tax law changes](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)
  - [Jess Holt interview provided valuable SMB insights for future content](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=165.0)
  - [Publishing delayed due to inaccuracies from recent tax bill; need efficient review process](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)

### Review Process Refinement

  - [New steps: tax review → final review (Pier) → ready to publish](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=619.0)
  - [Airtable tracker to be updated with new review steps](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [Automation to be set up for Slack notifications at each review stage](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)
  - [GrowthX to handle CMS upload and publishing once approved](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)

### Upcoming Content

  - [2 new blog posts to be ready by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
  - [Aiming to publish at least 2-3 blog posts next week after review process](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Continuing with 2 posts this week, 4 next week as planned](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=567.0)

### Future Content Ideas

  - [Industry-specific tax playbooks (e.g., real estate, medical practice, startups)](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=888.0)
  - [SMB cohort content](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=961.0)
  - [Tax risk assessment tool/quiz](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=834.0)
  - [Aida to send examples for reference](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)

### CMS Migration and CTAs

  - [Potential migration to Sanity CMS in coming months](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=996.0)
  - [Discussion on implementing customizable CTAs and lead magnets within blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
  - [Example shown from WebStacks using Sanity for easy CTA integration](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Aida to update Airtable with new review steps and set up Slack automation](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=1247.0)
  - [Pier to streamline internal tax review and approval process](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [Continue generating blog posts as planned (2 this week, 4 next)](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
  - [Aim to publish 2-3 blog posts next week after thorough review](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Aida to send examples of industry playbooks and assessment tools](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)
  - [Pier to consider new content ideas and CTA implementation for future CMS](https://fathom.video/share/VCzEZoyVwHtpiaEPQzDbTQPuw6fNiPHk?tab=summary&timestamp=1147.0)

action_items.description: Update startup tax deduction blog post for new voice/tone guidelines
Develop more efficient process for tax review & final approval of blog posts
Update Airtable w/ new review steps (tax review, final review). Set up Slack notifications for status changes
Complete 2 new blog posts by end of week for review
Send Pier examples of industry playbooks & tax risk assessments from other industries
